other_observers: Jim Beatty Jim's birding partner (I forget his name); I was not the original finder of this bird. Its presence had previously been reported on cobirds.
duration: one minute for the best looks
description: I observed the bird for one minute as it perched near the top of a snag, in excellent light.  Its underparts were a uniform, unstreaked brick red.  The back was more grayish than red.  The head was red, with a prominent gray eye patch, narrow in front of the eye, and flaring widely behind the eye.  The bill was thick and grayish.Behaviors: It flew up to near the top of a snag, perched there for a minute, and then flew off. It did not act like a flycatcher.Call: none heardPlumage: breeding plumage
